zh.lifeSummary: Joan de Kirk是de Kirk的女兒，於1340年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾柯克 明日香。她於1357年與Richard de Meynell在英國英格蘭德比夏爾柯克 明日香結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1398年在英國德比夏爾明日香 密爾享年58歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Kirk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish, English, and Danish: from Middle English, Danish kirk ‘church’ (Old Norse kirkja), a topographic name for someone who lived near a church. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century or earlier.

variants.name: Kurk
meanings: Dutch and German: metonymic occupational name for someone who deals with cork.
English (Gloucestershire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Kirk  . This surname is rare in Britain.
Turkish (Kürk): from kürk ‘fur’, applied as an ornamental name or as a nickname or metonymic occupational name for a furrier.

variants.name: Church
meanings: English: topographic name mostly found in southern and midland England, for someone who lived near a church, or possibly an occupational name for someone who worked at a church, such as a verger or sexton, from Middle English chirche ‘church’. The word comes from Old English cyrice, ultimately from medieval Greek kyrikon, for earlier kyriakōn (dōma) ‘(house) of the Lord’, from kyrios ‘lord’. Compare Kirk .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Kirch  .

variants.name: Kirt
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Kirk  . Compare   Kirkland   and   Kirtland  . This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Joan de Kirk was born in 1340, in Kirk Langley, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of de Kirk. She married Richard de Meynell in 1357, in Kirk Langley, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1398, in Langley Mill, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 58.

Elle est décédée en 1398, à Langley Mill, Derbyshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Kirkは1340年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアキルク ラングリーでde Kirkの娘として生まれた。1357年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアキルク ラングリーでRichard de Meynellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1398年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアラングリー ミルで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Kirk(은)는 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 커크 랭글리에서 de Kirk의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1357년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 커크 랭글리에서 Richard de Meynell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1398년 영국 더비실르 랭글리 미에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan de Kirk родилась в 1340 г. в местности кирк лангли, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. Её мать звали de Kirk. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de Meynell в 1357 г. в местности кирк лангли, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1398 г. в местности лангли милл, дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 58 лет.
